$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `folders` repo MINTED-VALUES path —
the generated-UUID remap + timestamp-placeholder normalization slice.
Unlike folders-tier2.json / tags-tier2.json (which pin every id + timestamp,
the zero-normalization form), these ops supply NO `options`, so both v4 and
the Rust port independently mint random UUIDs (crypto.randomUUID / uuid v4)
and wall-clock timestamps. The dumps therefore CANNOT match outright — they
are compared after normalization (see the harness test + cases file):
- id columns (`id`, `parentFolderId`) -> first-seen canonical tokens, rows
walked in natural-key (`path`) order, so FK RELATIONSHIPS are verified
per-row createdAt == updatedAt create-invariant asserted separately.
op[1] uses `parentFromOp: 0` — set parentFolderId to the id RETURNED by op 0.
This exercises a generated id referencing another generated id (the case the
remap exists for). Seed is empty so the only rows are the two minted ones.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is the same THROWAWAY key the other fixtures use.

ops.comment: Parent folder, minted id + timestamps (no options).
Child of op[0]; parentFolderId = the minted id returned by op[0].
